title: Directional Incident Context Authorization
scope: Authorization review only; no implementation, rendering, runtime behavior, user-facing wording, or ownership changes.
finalDetermination: DIRECTIONAL INCIDENT CONTEXT AUTHORIZED WITH OBSERVATIONS
authorizedFutureSurfaces: route-related wording surfaces that consume incident wording

runtimeCandidatePrototype.reusePath: source validation and fail-closed candidate eligibility pattern

serviceLayer.reusePath: service boundary for future incident wording consumer

consumerLayer.reusePath: consumer boundary; must remain wording-only and fail-closed

awarenessLayer.reusePath: do not reuse direct top-awareness rendering path; reuse only evidence and protections

staticReviewEvidence.refreshPortraitV2LocalizedIntelligence: js/app.js owns localized Awareness Brief refresh and writes top status primary/secondary.
staticReviewEvidence.gridlyV2TopStatusPrimary: index.html defines the top status primary element; js/gridlyDirectionalAwarenessLayer.js also targets it, which is prohibited for future directional incident context.
staticReviewEvidence.gridlyV2TopStatusSecondary: index.html defines the top status secondary element; js/gridlyDirectionalAwarenessLayer.js also targets it, which is prohibited for future directional incident context.
staticReviewEvidence.buildLocalizedIncidentLabel: js/app.js incident label builder is suitable for future optional directional enrichment.
staticReviewEvidence.buildRoadHazardDisplay: js/app.js road hazard display builder is suitable for future optional directional enrichment.
staticReviewEvidence.resolveGridlyAuthoritativeIncidentDispla: js/app.js authoritative location resolver is suitable as an eligibility checkpoint before adding direction.
staticReviewEvidence.gridlyDirectionalServiceConsumer: js/gridlyDirectionalServiceConsumer.js keeps directional labels unexposed and protected integrations disconnected.
staticReviewEvidence.userFacingRenderingEnabled: All reviewed Phase 1 runtime assets keep userFacingRenderingEnabled false.

limits: no direct Awareness Brief DOM writes
no runtime behavior change in V726

mergeRecommendation.quickSummary: Authorize future directional incident context with observations for US 90 and TX 146; keep FM 1960 deferred and all rendering disabled in V726.
mergeRecommendation.exactNextStep: Open V727 for fail-closed incident wording enrichment design behind explicit per-corridor rendering authorization.
